name: Tuvalu

visa_free.notes: 90 days in any 180-day period, all purposes except paid work, per the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement signed 1 July 2016 (OJ L 211, 6.8.2016). Applies to holders of ordinary, diplomatic, service/official or special passports. Ireland is explicitly excluded from this agreement. Covers Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, and Sweden.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement. Not an EU member but included in the agreement.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement.
90 days in any 180-day period as a Schengen associate state covered by the EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement.

90-day visa-free entry for Taiwan (Republic of China) passport holders per bilateral visa waiver agreement. Exact signing date is unconfirmed in official sources reviewed. Tuvalu and Taiwan maintain formal diplomatic relations.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visitor permit issued on arrival free of charge (Schedule 1 of Immigration Regulations 2014/2022). No employment, study or proselytising permitted.

Citizens of countries not listed in Schedule 1 receive a visitor permit on arrival upon payment of a fee of $100 (AUD), unless waived by the Minister. Permit is valid for up to 30 days, extendable for a further 30 days. Per Regulation 2(1)(b) of the Immigration Regulations 2014 (2022 Revised Edition). Ireland falls in this category as it is explicitly excluded from the EU-Tuvalu visa waiver agreement.

visa_policy.notes: Tuvalu's entry system issues visitor permits on arrival for all nationalities. Schedule 1 of the Immigration Regulations 2014 (unchanged in 2022 Revised Edition) lists approximately 37 nationalities/territories whose citizens are not liable to pay the AUD$100 visitor permit fee. All other nationalities pay AUD$100 on arrival. Maximum stay for all visitor permits is 30 days, extendable once for 30 days. The EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (signed 1 July 2016, OJ L 211, 6.8.2016) provides EU member state nationals (except Ireland), plus Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway and Switzerland 90 days in any 180-day period visa-free. Taiwan passport holders also receive 90 days visa-free per bilateral agreement (date unconfirmed). Serbia signed a mutual visa exemption agreement in November 2024, pending ratification. No e-visa or ETA system exists.

cbi.notes: No official Citizenship by Investment program exists in Tuvalu. The government briefly considered a CBI scheme around 2020-2022 but did not implement it. Tuvalu's laws historically allowed issuance of investor travel documents (not citizenship) in the 1990s, but this is no longer an active program. No official program found on gov.tv or any official government source.

agreements: EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement (2016) - bilateral, 90 days/180-day period for EU member states (except Ireland), Iceland, Liechtenstein, Norway, Switzerland
Tuvalu-Taiwan visa waiver agreement - 90 days visa-free for Taiwan passport holders (date unconfirmed)
Tuvalu-Serbia mutual visa exemption agreement (signed November 2024, pending ratification)

gaps: The Tuvalu government website (gov.tv) remains unreachable (ECONNREFUSED) — direct confirmation of current visa policy page not possible.
The Tuvalu High Commission website (tuvaluhc.org.nz/visa) has an expired SSL certificate and could not be accessed.

Taiwan visa waiver with Tuvalu is confirmed by Wikipedia and multiple travel aggregators but the exact bilateral agreement signing date and official source document could not be retrieved from mofa.gov.tw or any Tuvalu official domain.

Serbia-Tuvalu visa exemption agreement (signed November 2024) is referenced by Wikipedia only — ratification status and official text not confirmed from official government sources. Not included in visa_free as ratification is pending.

Full text of Schedule 1 from Immigration Regulations 2014 PDF could not be read directly (binary PDF); the country list is based on the 2014 PDF originally cited in the previous record and corroborated by Wikipedia.
No official source found confirming or denying any RBI or fast-track skilled worker program.
Australia-Tuvalu Falepili Union is an outbound mobility scheme for Tuvaluan citizens to move to Australia — not an inbound immigration program for Tuvalu.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS RESEARCH: Exhaustive search found no conditional entry rules for Tuvalu: no entry benefit based on holding a third-country visa or residence permit (US, UK, Schengen, Australia, Canada, Japan, GCC/UAE or any other issuer); no OCI or similar diaspora/heritage status carve-out; no diplomatic/service-passport-only bilateral visa waiver agreement distinct from the EU-Tuvalu agreement (which covers all passport types equally including ordinary).
validation_notes: CORRECTION 1: Added Taiwan to visa_free (90 days) — Taiwan passport holders receive 90-day visa-free entry per bilateral waiver; Taiwan was entirely absent from the prior record. Source: Wikipedia Visa policy of Tuvalu (corroborating multiple aggregators).

CORRECTION 2: Removed Denmark, Iceland, Norway, Sweden, and Switzerland from visa_on_arrival — these were incorrectly duplicated there. Denmark and Sweden are EU member states fully covered by the EU-Tuvalu 90-day waiver; Iceland, Norway, and Switzerland are Schengen associates also covered. Moving all five to visa_free at 90 days removes the conflicting/redundant 30-day entries.
CORRECTION 3: Added Liechtenstein to visa_free (90 days) — Liechtenstein is a Schengen associate covered by the EU-Tuvalu agreement; it was missing from the prior record entirely.
CORRECTION 4: Fixed EU visa_free entry description to specify 'except Ireland' — Ireland is explicitly excluded from the EU-Tuvalu visa waiver agreement, a material omission in the prior record.
CORRECTION 5: Added note that Ireland falls in the paid visa-on-arrival category ('All other nationalities' entry) since it is excluded from the EU waiver.
CORRECTION 6: Added Serbia agreement (November 2024, pending ratification) to the agreements list and notes; not added to visa_free as ratification is unconfirmed.
UNCHANGED: CBI, RBI, fast_track — no new official evidence of any program.

outbound_official.note: no official Tuvalu government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

sources_checked.description: Tuvalu Immigration Regulations 2014 - official PDF hosted on Tuvalu Permanent Mission to UN site. Contains Schedule 1 country list.
Immigration Regulations CAP 24.15.1 - 2022 Revised Edition from official Tuvalu Legislation website. Schedule 1 identical to 2014 version.

Tuvalu Permanent Mission to the UN - Consular Services page. States all visitors may enter for 30 days on arrival with valid passport, return ticket, and proof of funds. Confirms no visa required for stays up to 30 days for any nationality.

EU External Action Service announcement of EU-Tuvalu visa waiver agreement signing (1 July 2016). Confirms 90 days/180-day period for EU nationals, covering ordinary, diplomatic, service/official and special passports.
Full text of EU-Tuvalu Short-Stay Visa Waiver Agreement from EUR-Lex. Article 3 confirms coverage of ordinary, diplomatic, service, official and special passport holders equally.
Official Tuvalu Government immigration page - unreachable (ECONNREFUSED) during this validation.
Tuvalu High Commission visa page - expired SSL certificate, inaccessible.
Wikipedia Visa policy of Tuvalu - non-official but used to identify Taiwan 90-day visa-free entry and Serbia agreement (pending ratification). Neither confirmed via Tuvalu official domain.
Taiwan Bureau of Consular Affairs - Visa-Exempt Entry page. Confirms Tuvalu passport holders enter Taiwan visa-free for 90 days (reciprocal indicator of Taiwan-Tuvalu agreement).
